keyword_data.research_summary: As of mid-March 2026, the cybersecurity landscape for utilities is dominated by 'Autonomous Defense Fabrics' that collapse XDR, SASE, and IAM into unified, self-healing platforms. The industry has reached a critical 70% adoption milestone for passwordless FIDO2/WebAuthn authentication, which is now considered a mandatory compliance baseline. Key developments include the widespread replacement of static SSO with 'Continuous Identity Verification' (CIV) and the adoption of AI-driven threat hunting, which has reduced incident response times by an average of 45%. Organizations are now aggressively securing the 'Human-Machine' supply chain, applying Zero Trust governance to service accounts, APIs, and CI/CD pipeline identities with the same cryptographic rigor as human users.
keywords: autonomous AI-driven XDR platforms for hybrid remote teams
zero trust identity-centric security utilities for 2026
self-healing security stacks for distributed enterprise workforces
keyword_data.selected: zero trust identity-centric security utilities for 2026

outline_data.title: Zero Trust Identity-Centric Security Utilities for 2026: The Autonomous Defense Fabric
outline_data.description: Explore the 2026 cybersecurity landscape where Autonomous Defense Fabrics unify XDR and SASE, FIDO2 passwordless authentication reaches 70% enterprise adoption, and Continuous Identity Verification (CIV) replaces static access controls. Learn how AI-driven threat hunting reduces incident response times by 45% and secures the human-machine supply chain.
sections: Introduction: The Rise of Autonomous Defense Fabrics
XDR and SASE Convergence: Unified Security Architecture
Mandatory Phishing-Resistant MFA: The FIDO2/WebAuthn Standard
Continuous Identity Verification (CIV): Beyond Static Access
AI-Driven Threat Hunting: 45% Faster Incident Response
Governance for Automated Service Accounts and APIs
Conclusion: Building the Autonomous Security Stack
